2606.08841.title: ZIPP:Zero-shot Image Personalization from Personas
2606.08841.paper_summary: Text-to-image diffusion models are increasingly deployed in open-ended creative contexts, yet their outputs remain impersonal, optimized for aggregate aesthetics rather than individual taste. Human preferences are pluralistic: one user favoring muted, nostalgic portraits may prefer vibrant street photography, while another gravitates toward dreamy film aesthetics. Existing methods require dense interaction histories or per-user fine-tuning, failing in cold-start settings and collapsing context-dependent preferences into a static representation. We introduce zero-shot image personalization from personas (ZIPP), which conditions image generation on natural-language personas (concise descriptors of a user's identity and aesthetic sensibilities) without any user-specific data or weight updates. ZIPP uses an LLM to rewrite prompts from the perspective of a given persona, steering diffusion models toward personalized outputs. To mine personas at scale, we train an inductive Graph Attention Network over a 22M-user Reddit interaction graph with dual contrastive objectives aligning graph structure with visual behavior, then verbalize learned representations into natural-language personas via an MLLM. We introduce ZIPBench, the first zero-shot personalization benchmark with 1.5K users, graph-mined personas, and 40K generated images. Across four benchmarks and 14 LLMs spanning five model families, persona conditioning yields consistent gains (13-20%), with frontier models benefiting most. In the few-shot setting, ZIPP matches or exceeds fine-tuned baselines trained on 100+ examples per user. ZIPP achieves the lowest preference distributional divergence (CMMD 0.16 vs. 0.55), and IPF-normalized demographic evaluation shows it substantially reduces subpopulation bias present in existing methods. Human evaluation confirms a 79% win rate over generic generation and 58-65% over all fine-tuned baselines.
2606.08841.paper_summary_zh: 文本到圖像的擴散模型越來越多地應用於開放式創意環境中，然而它們的輸出仍然缺乏個性，優化的是整體美學而非個人品味。人類的偏好是多元的：一位用戶可能偏愛柔和、懷舊的肖像，而另一位則可能更喜歡生動的街頭攝影，還有一位則傾向於夢幻的電影美學。現有的方法需要密集的互動歷史或針對每個用戶的微調，這在冷啟動環境中失效，並將依賴上下文的偏好壓縮成靜態表示。我們提出了基於角色的零樣本圖像個性化（ZIPP），它在沒有任何用戶特定數據或權重更新的情況下，基於自然語言角色（用戶身份和美學感知的簡潔描述）來調節圖像生成。ZIPP使用大型語言模型（LLM）從給定角色的角度重寫提示，將擴散模型引導至個性化的輸出。為了大規模挖掘角色，我們在一個2200萬用戶的Reddit互動圖上訓練了一個歸納圖注意網絡，並使用雙重對比目標來對齊圖結構與視覺行為，然後通過多語言大型模型（MLLM）將學到的表示轉化為自然語言角色。我們推出了ZIPBench，第一個零樣本個性化基準，包含1500名用戶、圖挖掘的角色和40K生成的圖像。在四個基準和涵蓋五個模型系列的14個LLM中，角色調節帶來了一致的增益（13-20%），最前沿的模型受益最大。在少量樣本的設置中，ZIPP的表現與基於100多個樣本的微調基準相當或超過。ZIPP實現了最低的偏好分佈差異（CMMD 0.16對比0.55），而IPF標準化的人口評估顯示它顯著減少了現有方法中存在的子群體偏見。人類評估確認其在通用生成上有79%的勝率，並在所有微調基準上有58-65%的勝率。
2606.08841.author: Harini SI et.al.
2606.08841.authors: Harini SI, Somesh Singh, Yaman Kumar Singla, David Doermann, Rajiv Ratn Shah
